Overfitted moralities are external assessments of people's moral systems which are overfitted, that is to say they attribute errors the person makes as being intentional elements of their moral systems

As with the [[Competence performance distinction]] there are essentially two elements of it

* random errors (performance) being seen as their moral values
* Competence errors being seen as their moral values

A model can attribute false depth based on performance errors by developing arbitrary concepts to explain such errors, while it tends to read competence errors which contradict the person's moral theory as being elements of the theory

[[Category:Psychology]]
[[Category:Pages]]
[[Category:Git synced pages]]
